page: Amazon Web Services r8i.8xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the r8i.8xlarge demonstrates strong performance in memory-bound and text-processing tasks. It achieves a top-tier result in Geekbench text processing and a strong score in single-core and multi-core Geekbench tests. Memory bandwidth tests show 133.67 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 63.0 GB/sec for large-block write operations. The server performs strongly in Redis SET operations, reaching 58.27 million operations per second, and shows strong throughput for static web serving at 55.37 GB/sec. For machine learning workloads, the CPU's AMX instructions enable top-tier text generation speeds of 404.39 tokens per second on small 135M LLM models, though performance drops to a weak 2.71 tokens per second on larger 70B models.

From a resource-density perspective, the r8i.8xlarge offers high memory capacity relative to its compute resources, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. The primary tradeoff is the complete reliance on network-attached storage, which may limit I/O-heavy database operations compared to instances with local SSDs. Additionally, the lack of a GPU limits its utility for heavy deep learning training, despite decent CPU-based inference on smaller models. This server is highly suited for high-throughput in-memory databases like Redis, memory-intensive data processing, enterprise web serving, and medium-scale text processing pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services r8i.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory (7 GHz) but lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on a 15 Gbps baseline network interface. The architecture supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, which drive strong performance in text processing, cryptography, and small-scale LLM inference. Benchmarks show strong capabilities in Redis SET operations (58.27 million operations/sec) and static web serving (55.37 GB/sec). However, text generation performance drops significantly on larger 70B models. This instance is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, including in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise web applications that benefit from a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio without requiring local NVMe storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 256 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for in-memory databases and high-throughput web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and Intel Xeon processors for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring high-speed DDR5 RAM and advanced Intel Xeon processors for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs and 256 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with AVX-512 and AMX extensions
Strong performance in Redis operations and static web serving
No local storage or dedicated GPU hardware included
15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for in-memory databases, caching, and text processing
